action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 16's 1-epoch smoke test succeeded, producing checkpoints_v3/best.pt with val_acc=0.6060 and val_macro_f1=0.3227. The training pipeline (mp.spawn DDP, AMP, class-weighted CE, RandAugment) is now stable. Our best test macro_f1 remains 0.4197 from checkpoints_v2/best.pt. Analysis: We have ~2.64 hours remaining. Based on prior timing, 10 epochs of CLIP ViT-L/14 take ~90 minutes, leaving ample time for evaluation. The 1-epoch checkpoint likely has a freshly initialized head (either linear or MLP) that needs more epochs to surpass the old 0.4197 baseline. Continuing training from checkpoints_v3/best.pt for 10 more epochs is the most efficient path. Decision: Write a clean resume script (continue_train.py) that loads checkpoints_v3/best.pt, reads its architecture from saved args, and trains for 10 epochs with cosine LR warmup, differential LR, label smoothing, and gradient clipping. Save the best checkpoint to checkpoints_v4/best.pt. Keep predict.py unchanged so we can still submit the old best if needed.
action.command: python -c "import torch; ckpt=torch.load('checkpoints_v3/best.pt', map_location='cpu'); print('Args:', ckpt.get('args', {})); print('Epoch:', ckpt.get('epoch', 'N/A')); print('Val F1:', ckpt.get('val_macro_f1', 'N/A')); print('Last keys:', list(ckpt['model_state_dict'].keys())[-5:])" && python continue_train.py --checkpoint checkpoints_v3/best.pt --output_dir checkpoints_v4 --epochs 10 --batch_size 64 --num_workers 4 --lr_backbone 1e-6 --lr_head 1e-4
